reasoning:
The forecast asks whether TRV's close price on two specific dates (Feb 8 and Mar 3) will physically exceed its close price on Feb 1, 2026. Since Feb 1 is a Sunday, the threshold price will be the Jan 30 close of 283.47. 
First, I checked for mechanical price drags, specifically the Q1 dividend. Historical and recent announcements show that TRV's ex-dividend date falls on March 10, 2026. Since this is AFTER both resolution dates, there will be no mechanical $1.10 drop dragging on the stock's price for these targets.
Second, regarding price action, TRV recently rallied over $13 from mid-January lows, closing at nearly an all-time high of 283.47. Over shorter time scales like 1 week (to Feb 8), stock price changes resemble a random walk with high noise. A statistical linear model gives a roughly 48% chance of the stock remaining above the threshold for Feb 8, factoring in a slight possibility of near-term mean reversion after a steep vertical climb. Over a slightly longer period (to Mar 3), the inherent upward drift of equities offsets the mean reversion, increasing the probability closer to slightly above baseline. Thus, I estimate probabilities of 49% and 52%, aligning with market efficiency and a minor positive drift.
